title: OpenAI abandons yet another side quest: ChatGPT’s erotic mode
summary:
OpenAI has decided to indefinitely pause the development of an 'erotic mode' for ChatGPT, a feature that had sparked significant controversy since its initial proposal by CEO Sam Altman in October. The decision comes amid growing concerns from tech watchdog groups and internal discussions within the company, where advisors expressed fears about the potential implications of such a feature. The backlash culminated in a heated meeting in January, highlighting the ethical dilemmas surrounding AI's role in sensitive topics. As a result, the timeline for the release of this adult mode has been scrapped entirely, with no further comments from OpenAI on the matter.

This move is part of a broader strategy by OpenAI to consolidate its focus on core business objectives. In recent days, the company has also announced the deprioritization of Instant Checkout, a feature aimed at transforming ChatGPT into an e-commerce platform, and the shutdown of Sora, its AI video generator. These decisions reflect a strategic pivot to eliminate distractions and concentrate on serving business users and developers more effectively.

The timing of these changes appears to be influenced by increasing competition from companies like Anthropic, which have been rapidly advancing in the AI space with new coding and business tools. OpenAI's shift indicates a recognition of the need to streamline operations and prioritize features that align more closely with its long-term goals, ensuring that it remains competitive in a fast-evolving industry landscape.
social_text: OpenAI has paused its controversial erotic mode for ChatGPT, citing internal and external criticism. This follows the deprioritization of Instant Checkout and the shutdown of Sora, as the company shifts focus to core business users and coding tools.

title: Data centers get ready — the Senate wants to see your power bills
summary:
In a significant move against the rising energy consumption of data centers, U.S. Senators Josh Hawley and Elizabeth Warren have urged the U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) to implement mandatory annual reporting for these facilities. Their letter highlights the urgent need for standardized data on energy use, emphasizing that the current lack of reliable information poses risks to effective grid planning and oversight. As electricity demand continues to surge, particularly from data centers, the senators are advocating for greater transparency and accountability in energy consumption.

The push for regulation comes amid a broader political effort to address the environmental impact of data centers. Senators Bernie Sanders and Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez have also announced plans to introduce legislation that would temporarily halt new data center construction until a regulatory framework for artificial intelligence can be established. This reflects growing concerns about the sustainability of energy use in the tech sector, especially as data centers are projected to nearly triple their energy demand by 2035.

The EIA, established in 1977, plays a crucial role in collecting and analyzing energy data in the U.S. However, its current reporting categories are too broad to effectively capture the specific energy demands of data centers. As the energy landscape evolves, the senators' call for more detailed reporting could pave the way for more informed policy decisions and help mitigate the environmental impact of the tech industry’s rapid growth.
social_text: Senators Josh Hawley and Elizabeth Warren are pushing for mandatory annual reporting on data center energy use, citing risks to grid planning as demand surges. Energy consumption from data centers is expected to triple by 2035.

title: Defense startup Shield AI lands $12.7B valuation, up 140%, after US Air Force deal
summary:
Shield AI, an autonomous military aircraft manufacturer, has achieved a remarkable $12.7 billion valuation following a successful Series G funding round that raised $1.5 billion. This valuation marks a staggering 140% increase from its previous valuation of $5.3 billion just a year prior. The funding round was led by Advent International, a private equity firm with a significant focus on defense technology, alongside a group from JPMorgan Chase. Additionally, Shield AI secured $500 million in preferred shares from Blackstone and arranged a $250 million loan, further bolstering its financial position.

The capital raised will facilitate Shield AI's acquisition of Aechelon Technology, a company specializing in flight simulation technology for U.S. military pilot training. This strategic move is expected to enhance Shield AI's capabilities in developing advanced autonomous systems. The funding surge comes on the heels of Shield AI's selection by the U.S. Air Force for its Collaborative Combat Aircraft drone prototype program, a significant endorsement of its Hivemind autonomy software. This selection underscores the growing importance of advanced autonomous technologies in modern military operations.

In a notable collaboration, Shield AI's software will be utilized alongside Anduril's Lattice system for the Air Force's Fury autonomous fighter jet. This partnership reflects the Air Force's strategy to avoid vendor lock-in by integrating multiple technologies into its next-generation drone fleet. Despite the competitive landscape, Anduril continues to thrive, having recently raised $2.5 billion at a $30.5 billion valuation and is reportedly seeking to raise up to $8 billion at a projected $60 billion valuation. The developments in this sector signal a robust investment trend in defense technology, driven by the increasing demand for innovative military solutions.
social_text: Shield AI's valuation skyrocketed to $12.7B after raising $1.5B in Series G funding, a 140% increase in one year. The U.S. Air Force selected its Hivemind software for drone prototypes, enhancing military capabilities.

title: Circle shares tank 20%. But traders are reading the Clarity Act wrong, says Bernstein
summary:
Circle's shares experienced a significant drop of nearly 20% on Tuesday, leading to a broader decline in crypto stocks. This downturn was primarily triggered by investor concerns regarding a proposed ban on stablecoin yield, as outlined in the latest version of the Clarity Act. This legislation aims to establish a regulatory framework for the cryptocurrency market in the United States, a long-awaited development that has garnered much attention. The proposed ban would prevent companies from offering yields on stablecoins akin to interest on traditional bank deposits, raising fears among investors about the future profitability of stablecoin issuers like Circle.

However, analysts from Bernstein, including Gautam Chhugani, argue that the market has misinterpreted the implications of the Clarity Act. They clarify that while Circle earns yield, it is Coinbase that distributes it, suggesting that the legislation primarily targets distribution rather than the earning mechanisms of stablecoin issuers. Bernstein's analysis indicates that there may still be opportunities for platforms to offer incentives through activity-based rewards, which could mitigate the negative impact of the proposed regulations. This perspective suggests that the market's reaction may have been overly pessimistic.

The Clarity Act represents a significant step toward establishing a coherent regulatory environment for cryptocurrencies, with bipartisan support from key senators. Patrick Witt, from the President’s Council of Advisors for Digital Assets, acknowledged the efforts of Senators Thom Tillis and Angela Alsobrooks in advancing this legislation. While the proposed regulations present challenges, they also open avenues for legitimate activities tied to stablecoins, such as payments and trading, potentially allowing for continued growth in the sector despite regulatory hurdles. As the legislative process unfolds, further clarity on the implications for the crypto market will be crucial for investors and stakeholders alike.
social_text: Circle shares fell 20% amid fears of a stablecoin yield ban in the Clarity Act. Bernstein analysts argue the market misreads risks, as activity-based rewards may still be allowed, indicating potential for recovery.

title: I Have $2.2 Million Invested and Pay a 1% Advisor Fee. Is That Too High?
summary:
In the realm of financial advisory services, the fee structures employed by advisors can significantly impact the overall investment returns for clients. Typically, advisors charge a percentage of the assets they manage, with 1% being a common rate. For instance, if an advisor manages $100,000, they would charge $1,000 annually. This percentage-based fee structure is prevalent, but it can vary based on performance benchmarks or commission-based transactions. Performance fees reward advisors for exceeding specific financial goals, while commission structures compensate them for each transaction executed on behalf of the client.

Additionally, financial advisors may offer hourly rates or fixed fees for specific services, such as tax preparation or general financial planning. The hourly rate is often billed in six-minute increments, which is standard practice in the professional services sector. This fee-for-service model allows clients to pay only for the services they require, providing flexibility in how they engage with their advisors. Many advisors utilize a combination of these fee structures to cater to diverse client needs.

For an individual with $2.2 million in assets, a 1% advisory fee translates to an annual cost of $22,000. While this fee is within the industry average, it raises questions about value and service quality. Clients should evaluate their advisor's performance, the services provided, and whether the fee aligns with their financial goals. Engaging with a fiduciary advisor can help clients navigate these considerations and ensure they receive appropriate guidance tailored to their financial situation.
social_text: With $2.2 million managed at a 1% fee, you're paying $22,000 annually for advisory services. Evaluate if this aligns with your financial goals and the value provided by your advisor.

title: Kenya’s flower industry loses millions of dollars weekly due to the Iran war
summary:
Kenya's flower industry is facing significant financial challenges due to the ongoing conflict in Iran, which has led to weekly losses of approximately $1.4 million. The Kenya Flower Council reported that over the past three weeks, the industry has suffered losses exceeding $4.2 million. The conflict has caused a decline in demand and severe disruptions in shipping, which are critical for the timely export of flowers. The situation has been exacerbated by increased freight costs and logistical delays, making it difficult for growers to maintain their usual export volumes.

The impact on individual farms is stark. For instance, Isinya Flower Farms, located just south of Nairobi, has seen its daily exports plummet from 450,000 stems to between 150,000 and 200,000 stems, resulting in a staggering 50% reduction in output. This decline is particularly concerning given that the Middle East typically accounts for about 30% of their business. Although Europe remains the largest market for Kenyan flowers, the conflict has disrupted cargo freight routes, further complicating the situation for growers.

As freight rates soar to unprecedented levels, with charges reaching $5 per kilo—double the normal rate—many customers are unable to purchase flowers. The combination of high costs and limited freight options has left growers like Anantha Kumar of Isinya Flower Farms warning of a potential crisis in the industry. With the horticulture sector valued at over $800 million annually, these losses could have far-reaching implications for Kenya's economy if the situation does not improve soon.
social_text: Kenya's flower industry is losing $1.4M weekly due to the Iran war, with over $4.2M lost in 3 weeks. Exports have dropped by 50%, and freight costs have surged to $5/kg, impacting demand and logistics.

title: Goldman Sachs says crypto prices may have bottomed and these stocks have attractive setups
summary:
Goldman Sachs has indicated that cryptocurrency prices may have reached a bottom after a prolonged period of decline, suggesting a potential recovery in the market. According to analyst James Yaro, stocks associated with the crypto sector have experienced a significant downturn, dropping 46% since their peak in October 2025. Despite this, Yaro believes that the current market conditions present an attractive entry point for investors, particularly in companies that are less directly tied to the volatile nature of crypto prices. He emphasizes that valuations are becoming more appealing, especially for selective investments within the digital asset space.

The report highlights three key stocks that Goldman Sachs recommends: Robinhood, Figure Technologies, and Coinbase. Figure Technologies, which specializes in blockchain-based home equity line of credit (HELOC) origination and sales, has shown strong performance and is expected to continue expanding. Goldman Sachs has raised its price target for Figure from $39 to $42, indicating a potential upside of 35% from its recent closing price of $31. Robinhood is also noted for its efforts to attract more advanced traders by enhancing its offerings, while Coinbase is recognized for its growth potential in crypto derivatives trading and subscription services.

While Goldman Sachs has lowered its price targets for Robinhood and Coinbase, both companies still show promise for future growth. Yaro cautions that although prices may have stabilized, trading volumes could decline further, which may impact the overall market dynamics. The firm maintains a buy rating on all three stocks, reflecting confidence in their potential to rebound as the crypto market stabilizes and evolves.

title: The research firm whose AI paper knocked the whole stock market is out with another big call
summary:
Citrini Research, known for its impactful market predictions, has issued a new warning regarding the potential for an oil-driven economic slowdown. Founder James van Geelen highlighted that persistently high energy prices could significantly affect consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to a challenging environment for equities. He emphasized that geopolitical tensions, particularly ongoing conflicts, are contributing to sustained oil prices, which could further exacerbate the situation. Van Geelen's insights suggest that even if the Federal Reserve shifts towards rate cuts, the underlying economic conditions may still lead to a decline in stock values.

In a recent Substack post, van Geelen elaborated on how elevated oil prices function as a tax on growth, diminishing purchasing power and tightening financial conditions without requiring additional action from the Fed. He pointed out that with policy rates already near neutral, merely maintaining current rates could be restrictive enough to impact economic growth negatively. This perspective underscores a significant shift in the economic landscape, where external factors like energy prices play a critical role in shaping market dynamics.

Citrini's latest analysis builds on its reputation for contrarian views, following a previous bearish stance on the AI sector that suggested the technology boom could lead to increased unemployment. The firm’s current outlook indicates that if oil prices remain high, the resulting economic strain could lead to a broader slowdown, affecting equities and overall market sentiment. Investors and analysts alike will be closely monitoring these developments as they navigate the complexities of the current economic climate.

title: Iran war has altered the global natural gas market. Goldman says these 3 stocks will benefit
summary:
The ongoing U.S.-Iran war has significantly disrupted the global liquefied natural gas (LNG) market, with Goldman Sachs projecting these disruptions to persist until 2027. The conflict has particularly impacted Qatar, which accounts for approximately 3% of the world's LNG supply. Recent attacks from Iran have resulted in a loss of 17% of Qatar's LNG export capacity, leading to a tightening of supply and increased margins. Goldman analyst John Mackay noted that the average LNG margins are expected to be 200% higher from 2026 to 2028, indicating a robust market despite potential demand growth challenges due to rising prices.

Goldman Sachs has identified three companies that are likely to benefit from this new market dynamic. Venture Global is highlighted as a strong buy, with a 12-month price target of $18.50, reflecting a 17% upside from its recent close. The firm has raised its EBITDA estimates for Venture Global by an average of 62% for the years 2026 through 2028, citing a clearer path to medium-term growth and financial stability. Additionally, Cheniere Energy is also positioned favorably, with a buy rating and a price target of $312, suggesting a nearly 9% gain. The company's strong balance sheet allows for significant stock buybacks, enhancing shareholder value.

Lastly, Golar LNG is rated as a buy with a price target of $60, indicating a potential gain of over 13%. The company is poised for growth with the commissioning of a fourth floating LNG vessel, which could further boost its earnings. Overall, the geopolitical landscape has created a unique opportunity for these companies to capitalize on the evolving LNG market, as they navigate the challenges posed by the ongoing conflict and the resulting supply constraints.
social_text: Goldman Sachs predicts LNG market disruptions from the U.S.-Iran war will last until 2027, benefiting Venture Global, Cheniere Energy, and Golar LNG with significant price targets and growth potential amid rising LNG margins.

title: Apple made strides with iOS 26 security, but leaked hacking tools still leave millions exposed to spyware attacks
summary:
Recent developments in iOS security have highlighted significant vulnerabilities that could expose millions of iPhone users to spyware attacks. Despite Apple's ongoing efforts to enhance security with the introduction of iOS 26 and features like Lockdown Mode, researchers from Google, iVerify, and Lookout have uncovered widespread hacking campaigns utilizing leaked tools such as Coruna and DarkSword. These tools have been employed by cybercriminals, including Russian spies and Chinese hackers, to target users who have not updated their devices to the latest software. The ease of access to these hacking tools poses a serious threat, as they can be used to exploit older iPhones that lack the latest security measures, leaving many users vulnerable to data theft and privacy breaches.

Apple has made substantial investments in security technologies, including the implementation of memory-safe code and the introduction of Memory Integrity Enforcement for the latest iPhone models. These advancements aim to fortify the iPhone against common exploitation techniques used by spyware. However, the existence of a significant number of outdated devices creates a dual security landscape, where users on the latest iOS enjoy enhanced protection while those on older versions remain at risk. This disparity underscores the importance of regular software updates and the need for users to remain vigilant against potential threats.

As the cybersecurity landscape continues to evolve, the implications of these findings are profound. The leaked hacking tools not only empower malicious actors but also challenge the narrative that iPhones are inherently secure. Apple must continue to innovate and address these vulnerabilities to maintain user trust and ensure the safety of its devices. The ongoing battle between cybersecurity advancements and the tactics employed by cybercriminals highlights the critical need for users to stay informed and proactive in safeguarding their digital lives.
social_text: Recent research reveals that leaked hacking tools like Coruna and DarkSword are targeting outdated iPhones, exposing millions to spyware. Apple’s iOS 26 offers enhanced security, but older devices remain vulnerable.

title: PepsiCo Leans on Local Sourcing and AI to Navigate Cost Pressures
summary:
PepsiCo, Inc. is adapting to current geopolitical and cost pressures by emphasizing local sourcing, with approximately 95% of its ingredients sourced from local suppliers. This strategy, articulated by Asia Pacific CEO Anne Tse, not only enhances the resilience of PepsiCo's supply chain but also fosters strong relationships with local growers, particularly for key ingredients like potatoes. By focusing on local sourcing, the company aims to mitigate risks associated with global supply chain disruptions while ensuring the freshness and quality of its products.

In addition to local sourcing, PepsiCo is leveraging artificial intelligence (AI) to streamline its operations across various markets, including China. The application of AI in precision agriculture, manufacturing management, and consumer analytics allows the company to enhance operational efficiency and expand production capacity without a proportional increase in workforce. This innovative approach positions PepsiCo to respond effectively to market demands while maintaining cost control, even as it continues to hire in regions where it is expanding its facilities.

Moreover, PepsiCo is committed to evolving its product offerings to meet changing consumer preferences, particularly in China. The company is focusing on developing premium, nutrient-dense products by reducing sugar and sodium levels and incorporating local ingredients that resonate with regional tastes. This strategic pivot not only aligns with health-conscious consumer trends but also reinforces PepsiCo's dedication to sustainability and local engagement, ensuring its long-term growth and relevance in the competitive food and beverage industry.
social_text: PepsiCo sources 95% of its ingredients locally, enhancing supply chain resilience. AI optimizes operations in China, boosting efficiency in agriculture and manufacturing, while the company focuses on healthier, premium products.

title: Morgan Stanley Updates NextEra Energy (NEE) Target on Load Growth and Data Center Demand
summary:
Morgan Stanley has raised its price target for NextEra Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE) from $106 to $110, maintaining an Overweight rating on the stock. This adjustment is based on the company's strong performance in the utility sector, which has outpaced the S&P 500 in February. The firm highlighted positive trends in load growth and the increasing demand for energy from data centers, which are becoming critical as digital infrastructure expands. The outlook for utilities remains optimistic, with NextEra positioned to capitalize on these growth opportunities.

In a significant development, NextEra Energy has secured land in Texas for a new gas-fired power plant that will support a large data center campus. This initiative is part of a broader agreement between the United States and Japan, with NextEra set to develop two major gas plants in Texas and Pennsylvania, totaling approximately 10 gigawatts of capacity. These projects are essential to meet the rising electricity demands associated with data centers, which are increasingly vital in today's digital economy.

CEO John Ketchum announced these developments at the CERAWeek conference in Houston, emphasizing the strategic importance of these projects. The Texas facility is expected to exceed 5 gigawatts in capacity, while the Pennsylvania site is still in the planning stages, requiring additional land and permits. This collaboration between the U.S. and Japan, involving $33 billion in investments, underscores NextEra's pivotal role in the energy sector and its commitment to supporting the growing needs of data-driven industries.
social_text: Morgan Stanley raises NextEra Energy's target to $110, citing load growth and data center demand. The $33B U.S.-Japan project will add 10GW capacity, enhancing energy supply for critical infrastructure.

title: 'Magnificent 7' stocks are seeing a shocking run: Chart
summary:
The 'Magnificent Seven' stocks, which include major tech players like Microsoft, Google, Amazon, and Meta, are currently experiencing a significant downturn, with each stock down double-digit percentages from their 52-week highs. Microsoft, in particular, has seen a decline of over 30% following a quarter marked by slowing sales growth and substantial capital expenditure commitments. Analysts, including Kenny Polcari, suggest that the current market conditions may not be favorable for investing in these stocks, indicating potential further downside risk.

Several factors contribute to the sell-off of the Magnificent Seven. Rising oil prices, attributed to Operation Epic Fury, have reignited inflation concerns, prompting the Federal Reserve to adopt a prolonged higher interest rate stance. This environment is particularly challenging for growth-oriented tech valuations, as higher rates diminish the present value of future earnings. Additionally, the substantial capital expenditures planned for AI infrastructure by major tech firms, projected to exceed $650 billion by 2026, have raised concerns about profit margins and investor sentiment.

Moreover, institutional investors are shifting their focus away from digital growth stocks towards more stable investments in energy, defense, and domestic manufacturing. This trend reflects a broader market rotation as investors seek perceived safe havens amid economic uncertainty. While some analysts believe the Magnificent Seven may eventually transition into value plays, the current sentiment suggests caution, with many investors preferring to explore alternative investment opportunities in the current climate.
social_text: The Magnificent Seven stocks are down double-digit percentages from their 52-week highs, with Microsoft down over 30%. Rising oil prices and high capital expenditures are pressuring tech valuations, prompting a shift to safer investments.

title: Mark Cuban Calls $30,000 Corporate Healthcare Burden 'Insane,' Backs Bill To Break Up Big Medicine Giants UNH, CVS, CI
summary:
Mark Cuban, the billionaire entrepreneur and founder of Cost Plus Drugs, has raised concerns about the escalating costs of corporate healthcare, which he claims are a significant factor behind layoffs in various industries. Cuban highlighted that employers are facing an average burden of $30,000 per family annually for healthcare premiums and services, labeling this financial strain as 'insane.' He argues that the majority of these costs are funneled to large, vertically integrated insurance companies, which complicate the billing process and obscure the true expenses incurred by employers. This situation has led to a misattribution of job losses to technological advancements like artificial intelligence, rather than the unsustainable healthcare costs that companies are grappling with.

In response to these challenges, Cuban is advocating for the 'Break Up Big Medicine Act,' a bipartisan legislative effort spearheaded by Senators Josh Hawley and Elizabeth Warren. This proposed bill aims to dismantle the monopolistic practices of major healthcare conglomerates such as UnitedHealth Group, CVS Health, and Cigna Group. By prohibiting common ownership between insurers, pharmacy benefit managers, and medical providers, the legislation seeks to foster competition and transparency in the healthcare market. Cuban's call to action emphasizes the need for systemic change to alleviate the financial pressures on employers and improve the overall healthcare landscape.

Cuban's stance reflects a growing frustration among business leaders regarding the impact of healthcare costs on employment and economic stability. As healthcare expenses continue to rise, the potential for job cuts and reduced hiring becomes increasingly pronounced. By rallying support for the 'Break Up Big Medicine Act,' Cuban hopes to initiate a critical dialogue about the future of healthcare in America and its implications for businesses and employees alike. His advocacy underscores the urgent need for reform in an industry that has long been characterized by consolidation and rising costs.
social_text: Mark Cuban highlights the staggering $30k annual healthcare cost per family, urging support for the Break Up Big Medicine Act to dismantle giants like UNH, CVS, and CI, aiming to reduce employer burdens and job cuts.

title: Deutsche Bank Boosts Chubb (CB) Target, Company Unveils Marine Insurance Partnership
summary:
Deutsche Bank has recently increased its price target for Chubb Limited (NYSE:CB) from $304 to $330, maintaining a Hold rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects the bank's updated estimates and the introduction of a forecast extending to 2028. Chubb, recognized as a Dividend King and Aristocrat, continues to demonstrate its strong position in the insurance market, which is bolstered by its extensive global presence in 54 countries and territories. The firm offers a diverse range of insurance products, including property and casualty insurance, life insurance, and reinsurance, catering to both commercial and personal needs.

In a strategic move to enhance its offerings, Chubb announced a partnership with Safe Harbor Marinas, making it the preferred insurance provider for members across over 150 marinas. This partnership allows Chubb to provide its Masterpiece Select Recreational Marine Insurance, which is designed to offer comprehensive coverage tailored for marine activities. The policy includes features such as total loss settlement with no deductible, coverage for mechanical and electrical breakdowns, and rental reimbursement, showcasing Chubb's commitment to delivering value to its customers.

As Chubb continues to innovate and expand its service offerings, it remains a significant player in the insurance sector. While Deutsche Bank acknowledges the potential of Chubb as an investment, it also suggests that certain AI stocks may present greater upside potential with less risk. This highlights the competitive landscape in which Chubb operates, as investors weigh various opportunities in the market, particularly in the evolving tech-driven sectors.

title: Trump volatility fatigue: Retail traders aren't buying the dips like they used to
summary:
Retail investors have historically driven the buy-the-dip strategy, particularly in response to market fluctuations influenced by political events, notably those involving former President Donald Trump. However, recent trends indicate a significant decline in retail trading activity, with flows dropping to $3 billion in the week of March 19-25, well below the 12-month average of $6.8 billion. This downturn reflects a broader shift in investor sentiment as the stock market grapples with heightened volatility, primarily due to geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and rising oil prices. The S&P 500 has seen a 5% decline since the onset of the conflict, prompting retail traders to reassess their strategies and participation in the market.

The TACO strategy, which gained traction in 2025, capitalized on Trump's tendency to backtrack on aggressive trade policies, allowing investors to profit from temporary market dips. However, the current geopolitical climate has dampened the enthusiasm of retail traders, who previously thrived on such volatility. As the market experiences systematic deleveraging and cautious buying from institutional investors, retail participation has receded significantly. Vanda Research highlights that the week began with a notable lack of optimism among retail investors, culminating in net selling for the first time since November 2023, despite a market surge triggered by potential diplomatic discussions between the U.S. and Iran.

The shift in retail trading behavior underscores the impact of external factors on market dynamics and investor psychology. With the S&P 500 experiencing fluctuations amid ongoing geopolitical tensions, retail investors are now more hesitant to engage in buying opportunities that once characterized their trading strategies. This evolving landscape suggests that retail traders may need to adapt their approaches in response to the current market conditions, moving away from the buy-the-dip mentality that has defined their trading habits in recent years. As the situation develops, it will be crucial for investors to monitor both market trends and geopolitical events to navigate the complexities of the current trading environment.
social_text: Retail trading activity has plummeted, with flows at $3B vs. a $6.8B average. The S&P 500 is down 5% since the Middle East conflict began, marking a shift in retail investor sentiment and strategy.

title: This industry group is the star of 2026, besides energy. Trivariate says these stocks are a buy
summary:
The industrials sector has emerged as a surprising leader in the stock market for 2026, showcasing its best performance in 25 years. According to Trivariate Research, the State Street Industrial Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLI) has seen a rally of over 5% year-to-date, positioning it among the six S&P 500 sectors that have gained this year. This performance is particularly notable against the backdrop of the energy sector, which has surged by 37%. A significant portion of the top stocks in the industrials sector have also performed well, with 14 out of the top 20 stocks by market capitalization showing gains, including GE Vernova, Caterpillar, and Lockheed Martin, all of which have achieved double-digit increases in their stock prices. However, Trivariate cautions that sustaining this momentum may prove difficult for the industrials sector. The current forward price-to-earnings ratio stands at approximately 24, indicating that stocks may be overvalued following this year's rally. The sector's recent performance has been buoyed by improvements in the Purchasing Managers Index (PMIs), a key indicator of economic growth, which has positively impacted cyclical sectors. Yet, with 15 distinct sub-industries within the industrials sector, making broad predictions remains complex. Adam Parker, founder of Trivariate Research, emphasizes the necessity for upward earnings revisions to maintain the sector's outperformance, a challenge given the current market dynamics.

Trivariate has identified several stocks that may continue to thrive, focusing on those in the top quartile of 12-quarter trailing earnings growth and showing positive changes in forward earnings expectations. Among these, Howmet Aerospace stands out, having already increased by over 15% this year, with a majority of analysts rating it as a buy. As the industrials sector navigates these complexities, investors will need to closely monitor earnings revisions and market conditions to gauge future performance accurately.

title: Google is launching Search Live globally
summary:
Google has announced the global launch of its AI-powered conversational search feature, Search Live, which will now be available in over 200 countries and territories. Initially launched in July 2025, this innovative feature allows users to utilize their phone cameras to receive real-time assistance through interactive conversations. By leveraging visual context from the camera feed, Search Live enables users to ask questions about objects in their immediate environment, facilitating a more intuitive search experience. Prior to this expansion, the feature was limited to users in the U.S. and India, highlighting the significance of this rollout in enhancing accessibility to advanced AI technology worldwide.

The expansion of Search Live is made possible by Google's latest audio and voice model, Gemini 3.1 Flash Live, which enhances the naturalness and intuitiveness of conversations. Users can engage with the feature by simply tapping the Live icon within the Google app on their Android or iOS devices. This allows for a seamless interaction where users can ask questions aloud and receive audio responses, followed by the ability to ask follow-up questions. The integration of visual context not only enriches the user experience but also provides links to additional information, making it a powerful tool for real-time assistance.

In addition to Search Live, Google has also announced the expansion of its Live Translate feature for Google Translate on iOS. This feature, which provides real-time translations through headphones, is set to reach more countries, including Germany, Spain, France, Nigeria, Italy, the United Kingdom, Japan, Bangladesh, and Thailand. These advancements underscore Google's commitment to enhancing user experience through AI-driven solutions, making information more accessible and interactions more engaging across the globe.
social_text: Google expands Search Live globally, enabling real-time assistance in over 200 countries. Users can ask questions using their camera for intuitive responses, powered by Gemini 3.1 Flash Live. Live Translate also expands to iOS in multiple countries.

title: Silicon Valley’s two biggest dramas have intersected: LiteLLM and Delve
summary:
This week, a significant security incident involving LiteLLM, an open-source AI model access platform, has drawn attention in Silicon Valley. Developed by a Y Combinator graduate, LiteLLM has gained immense popularity, with downloads reaching up to 3.4 million per day. However, a serious malware issue was discovered by Callum McMahon, a research scientist at FutureSearch, who found that the malware infiltrated LiteLLM through a dependency. This malicious code was capable of stealing login credentials and spreading to other open-source packages, creating a cascading effect of security breaches. The malware's poor design led to a malfunction that ultimately alerted McMahon to its presence, highlighting the vulnerabilities present in widely-used open-source software.

In response to the incident, the LiteLLM development team has been working diligently to resolve the security issues and has managed to address the problem within hours of its discovery. Despite the rapid response, the situation raises questions about the security practices of open-source projects and the reliance on third-party dependencies. LiteLLM's website still claims compliance with major security certifications, SOC2 and ISO 27001, which were obtained through the startup Delve. This contradiction has sparked discussions on social media about the effectiveness of these certifications in ensuring software security.

The intersection of these two narratives—LiteLLM's malware incident and its certification through Delve—has captivated the tech community, drawing parallels to dramatic storylines often seen in fictional portrayals of Silicon Valley. As the situation unfolds, it serves as a reminder of the importance of rigorous security measures in the rapidly evolving tech landscape, particularly for open-source projects that are widely adopted and relied upon by developers worldwide.
social_text: LiteLLM, a popular AI model platform with 3.4M daily downloads, faced a severe malware breach that stole credentials via dependencies. Discovered by Callum McMahon, the issue was resolved swiftly, raising security concerns about open-source software.

title: EXCLUSIVE: Nordic Knots’ Fresh Investment Proves Rugs Are a Hot Bet
summary:
Nordic Knots, a Scandinavian rug brand founded in 2016, has secured a significant investment to enhance its global presence and product offerings. The company, which recently celebrated its 10th anniversary, aims to achieve over $100 million in revenue by 2026, reflecting its ambitious growth strategy. The investment round, which is close to $100 million, is led by Imaginary Ventures, a prominent consumer fund known for backing premium brands. Other investors include Creades, Iris Ventures, and Lauren Santo Domingo, co-founder of Moda Operandi. This influx of capital will support Nordic Knots in expanding its product line and solidifying its position in the competitive home decor market.

The brand has experienced remarkable growth, with sales surging by 85% in 2025 compared to the previous year. This impressive performance underscores the increasing demand for Nordic Knots' design-forward rugs and textiles. The founders, Liza Berglund Laserow and Fabian Berglund, have leveraged their backgrounds in interior design and advertising to create a unique brand identity that resonates with consumers. Their vision is to establish Nordic Knots as a leading name in the interior and home category, with a focus on quality and aesthetic appeal.

As Nordic Knots prepares for its next phase of growth, the investment will enable the company to enhance its product development and strengthen its brand positioning, particularly in the U.S., which is currently its largest market. The strategic partnership with Imaginary Ventures and other investors will provide the necessary resources to navigate the competitive landscape and capitalize on emerging opportunities in the home decor sector. With a clear roadmap for expansion, Nordic Knots is poised to become a dominant player in the global market for stylish and high-quality rugs.
social_text: Nordic Knots secures nearly $100 million in investment led by Imaginary Ventures to boost global expansion and product development, targeting $100 million in revenue by 2026 after 85% sales growth in 2025.

title: US fixed 30-year mortgage rate jumps to six-month high as Iran war drags on
summary:
The average rate for a 30-year fixed mortgage in the U.S. has surged to 6.38%, marking a six-month high and reflecting a significant increase from 6.22% just a week prior. This rise is attributed to escalating oil prices resulting from the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, which has raised inflation concerns. The increase in mortgage rates has now persisted for four consecutive weeks, complicating efforts by the Trump administration to enhance housing affordability during a critical spring selling season. As the housing market typically sees heightened activity in spring, these rising rates could dampen home sales, which are already under pressure from inflationary trends.

The conflict in Iran has had a direct impact on oil prices, which have surged over 30% since the war began in late February. This spike in oil prices has led to increased U.S. Treasury yields, which are closely linked to mortgage rates. The 30-year fixed mortgage rate had previously dipped to 5.98% just before the onset of the war, following directives from President Trump to Freddie Mac and Fannie Mae to expand their purchases of mortgage-backed securities. This strategic move aimed to stabilize the housing market but has been undermined by the recent geopolitical developments.

As the midterm elections approach in November, housing affordability is becoming a critical political issue. The rising mortgage rates could influence voter sentiment, as potential homebuyers face higher borrowing costs. With the current economic climate and inflationary pressures, the housing market's trajectory remains uncertain, and stakeholders are closely monitoring these developments to gauge their impact on home sales and the broader economy.
social_text: The average 30-year fixed mortgage rate in the U.S. has risen to 6.38%, its highest since September, driven by a 30% surge in oil prices due to the Iran conflict, raising inflation concerns and impacting housing affordability ahead of midterms.

title: XRP News: Ripple Just Joined Singapore’s MAS BLOOM Sandbox—Will This Help XRP Price?
summary:
Ripple has recently joined the Monetary Authority of Singapore's (MAS) BLOOM sandbox, a significant step for the company as it seeks to enhance its institutional credibility in the digital asset space. The BLOOM initiative, launched in October 2025, builds on MAS's Project Orchid, which aimed to explore the potential of a digital Singapore dollar. With 16 members, including major global banking and payment institutions, Ripple's inclusion signifies that its infrastructure has been vetted for supervised testing involving real trade flows. This rigorous evaluation is crucial given Singapore's stringent regulatory environment for digital assets, which adds weight to Ripple's credibility as a player in the financial technology sector.

The BLOOM sandbox is designed to facilitate the testing of stablecoins and tokenized assets for real cross-border payments. Ripple's collaboration with Unloq, a Singapore-based supply chain finance firm, is particularly noteworthy. Together, they are piloting a system called SC+, which automates payments through smart contracts triggered by predefined conditions, such as the arrival of cargo. This innovative approach significantly reduces the time required for payment processing, compressing traditional transaction times from several days to mere minutes. This efficiency is especially beneficial for smaller businesses that often face high fees in conventional trade finance.

While Ripple's participation in the BLOOM sandbox is a positive development for its reputation and the potential use of its RLUSD stablecoin, XRP holders are left wondering about the direct impact on the token's price, currently sitting at $1.40. The pilot program aims to demonstrate the viability of Ripple's technology in real-world applications, and if successful, it could bolster confidence in XRP as a settlement asset. As the financial landscape evolves, Ripple's strategic moves within such prestigious initiatives may play a crucial role in shaping its future and that of XRP in the competitive digital currency market.
social_text: Ripple joins Singapore's MAS BLOOM sandbox, enhancing its credibility in digital assets. Partnering with Unloq, they aim to streamline payments for trade finance, reducing transaction times from days to minutes, impacting XRP's future.

title: AXN expands Kentucky axle plant targeting heavy-duty truck OEMs
summary:
AXN Automotive Systems has announced the completion of an expansion at its manufacturing facility in Louisville, Kentucky, aimed at enhancing its production capabilities for heavy-duty truck original equipment manufacturers (OEMs). This strategic move is part of a broader initiative by its parent company, Randoncorp, to bolster U.S. manufacturing and improve supply chain reliability across North America. Currently employing around 55 workers, the plant is expected to increase its workforce to approximately 100 employees and ramp up production to 100,000 axles annually, catering to various trailer types including dry van, refrigerated, and flatbed trailers.

The expansion includes significant investments in advanced production technologies, such as an automated welding cell and a dedicated paint line, which will enhance assembly capacity and production speed. With an investment of nearly $15 million over the next decade, AXN aims to solidify its presence in the North American market and respond to the growing demand from trailer manufacturers and the aftermarket sector. This development aligns with a broader trend of manufacturers shifting production to the U.S. to mitigate supply chain disruptions.

Daniel Randon, president and CEO of Randoncorp, emphasized the company's commitment to U.S. manufacturing, stating that this investment will not only improve delivery times but also ensure a more reliable supply for customers. As trailer manufacturers increasingly localize their production, AXN's expansion is poised to meet the rising domestic freight demand for raw materials and components, further strengthening its competitive position in the market.
social_text: AXN Automotive Systems expands its Louisville plant with a $15M investment, increasing production to 100,000 axles annually and creating 45 new jobs. This move enhances supply chain reliability for heavy-duty truck OEMs.

title: Amex launches new Graphite Business Cash Unlimited Card: Earn unlimited 2% on every purchase
summary:
American Express has introduced the Graphite™ Business Cash Unlimited Card, aimed at business owners seeking straightforward cash back rewards. This new offering allows cardholders to earn an unlimited 2% cash back on all eligible purchases, making it an attractive option for those who prefer simplicity in their rewards structure. Additionally, the card provides a lucrative 5% cash back on flights and prepaid hotel bookings made through American Express Travel, enhancing its appeal for business travelers. With an annual fee of $295, the card is positioned to deliver significant value for businesses that frequently incur expenses across various categories.

The welcome offer for new cardholders is particularly noteworthy. By spending $50,000 within the first six months, users can earn $1,500 in cash back, translating to a monthly spending requirement of approximately $8,333. This ambitious target is designed to attract serious business spenders who can leverage the card's rewards to maximize their return on investment. The card also features no preset spending limit and offers flexibility with the Pay Over Time option, which can be beneficial for managing cash flow.

Overall, the Graphite™ Business Cash Unlimited Card represents a strategic move by American Express to cater to the needs of small business owners who value both cash back rewards and travel benefits. With its competitive rewards structure and substantial welcome offer, this card is set to compete effectively in the crowded business credit card market, providing a compelling choice for entrepreneurs looking to optimize their expenses and rewards.
social_text: American Express launches the Graphite™ Business Cash Unlimited Card, offering unlimited 2% cash back on all purchases and 5% on travel. New users can earn $1,500 cash back after spending $50,000 in 6 months.

title: Technical Assessment: Bullish in the Intermediate-Term
summary:
The current state of crude oil prices suggests a temporary equilibrium, which has alleviated some pressure on equity markets. Despite the ongoing tug-of-war in global markets, major indices have stabilized, marking a small victory for investors. The S&P 500 (SPX) has maintained a level similar to that of four days prior, indicating a pause in its downward trajectory. However, the index has faced challenges, notably closing below its 200-day moving average for five consecutive days, which raises concerns about its overall momentum. Yet, the SPX is only 39 points shy of this critical average, and a strong rally could quickly reverse the current trend.

Analyzing the SPX's performance, it recently closed just below its weekly lower Bollinger Band, a technical indicator that historically signals potential buying opportunities. Such occurrences are typically seen as bullish signals, except during significant market corrections or bear phases. The last notable buy signals were recorded in April 2025, suggesting that traders should remain vigilant for a potential rebound as the index approaches the lower band again.

In conclusion, while the current market conditions present a mixed picture, the stabilization of crude oil prices and the SPX's proximity to critical technical levels could set the stage for a bullish turnaround. Investors are advised to monitor these indicators closely, as a rally could restore confidence in the equity markets and lead to renewed upward momentum in the S&P 500. The interplay between energy prices and equity performance will be crucial in the coming weeks as market participants navigate this uncertain landscape.
social_text: Crude oil prices stabilize, easing pressure on equity markets. The S&P 500 is only 39 points below its 200-day average, with potential buy signals emerging as it approaches the lower Bollinger Band.

title: Dell is back on Josh Brown's Best Stocks list. Why more gains are ahead
summary:
Dell Technologies has made a notable return to Josh Brown's Best Stocks list after a challenging period. Initially included on September 29, the stock faced a downturn, falling below its 200-day moving average before Thanksgiving. This led to its removal from the list for three months as investors grew concerned about the capital expenditure boom in the AI sector. However, following a consolidation phase from November to March, Dell's fortunes changed as positive earnings reports from hyperscalers and Nvidia's announcement of a trillion-dollar backlog reignited investor interest.

The current market landscape shows that only a small fraction of the S&P 500, about 3%, is making new 52-week highs, with Dell being one of the exceptions. This resurgence highlights the importance of patience and strategic re-evaluation in trading. Many investors might abandon a stock after a setback, but those who can endure and reassess their positions often find opportunities for significant gains. Dell's recent performance exemplifies this principle, demonstrating that sometimes the best trades require multiple attempts.

As Dell continues to gain momentum, it is essential for investors to recognize the potential for further growth. The stock's return to the Best Stocks list signifies renewed confidence in its prospects, particularly in the context of the booming AI market. With the right approach and emotional maturity, investors may find that revisiting previously discarded stocks can lead to rewarding outcomes, making Dell a noteworthy consideration for those looking to capitalize on market trends.
social_text: Dell Technologies has re-emerged on Josh Brown's Best Stocks list after a consolidation phase, driven by positive AI sector momentum and Nvidia's trillion-dollar backlog, marking it as a key player in a market with only 3% of S&P 500 stocks hitting new highs.

title: The drone market is about to go parabolic because of AI and war. How to play it
summary:
The global drone market is poised for unprecedented growth, driven by escalating defense spending and advancements in artificial intelligence (AI). Analysts attribute this surge to rising geopolitical tensions, particularly the ongoing conflict in Iran, which has significantly increased demand for drones. Companies like AeroVironment and Kratos Defense & Security Solutions are at the forefront of this transformation, as drones become essential tools in modern warfare. According to Barclays analyst Zornitsa Todorova, drones represent a physical embodiment of AI, enhancing capabilities in navigation, sensing, autonomy, and decision-making. This integration of AI is not only revolutionizing drone technology but also reshaping military strategies worldwide.

The market is projected to double from approximately $20 billion in 2020 to over $40 billion by 2025, with forecasts suggesting it could reach $250 billion by 2035. This growth trajectory is fueled by the increasing adoption of low-cost, one-way swarm deployments, which have become a hallmark of modern defense strategies. The rise in drone patents, which have surged 45 times since 2012, underscores the rapid innovation occurring within the sector. As AI continues to be embedded in drone technology, the market is expected to solidify its position as a core growth engine of Physical AI, alongside autonomous vehicles.

Recent conflicts, particularly in Ukraine and the Middle East, have further highlighted the strategic importance of drones. Production in Ukraine is anticipated to escalate from around 800,000 units in 2023 to nearly 5 million by 2026, reflecting the urgent need for tactical and counter-unmanned aerial systems. Analysts predict that the ongoing Iran war will accelerate the build rates and stockpiling of munitions among the U.S. and its allies, further solidifying the drone market's pivotal role in contemporary defense operations. As the landscape evolves, investors are keenly watching how these developments will shape the future of the drone industry.
social_text: The drone market is set to soar, projected to grow from $20B in 2020 to $250B by 2035. AI advancements and geopolitical tensions, especially the Iran war, are driving this explosive growth, with drone production in Ukraine expected to rise to nearly 5M units by 2026.

title: This AI marketing stock is a top gainer today. Goldman says there's much more to go
summary:
Braze, an artificial intelligence marketing firm, has emerged as a significant player in the stock market, particularly following its recent performance on Wednesday. The company saw its shares surge by 19% after reporting fourth-quarter results that exceeded expectations. With a revenue of $205.2 million, Braze not only surpassed the FactSet consensus of $198.2 million but also provided a promising outlook for future earnings. The firm anticipates revenues between $884 million and $889 million through January 2027, which is notably higher than the $858.2 million forecasted by analysts. This positive trajectory has caught the attention of Goldman Sachs, which has reaffirmed its buy rating on the stock despite lowering its price target from $45 to $40. The new target still suggests a substantial upside of 122% from the stock's closing price on Tuesday.

Analyst Gabriela Borges from Goldman Sachs emphasized Braze's potential for sustained growth, attributing this to the company's ability to capitalize on the shortcomings of incumbent providers who are hindered by outdated systems. Borges noted that the recent quarter marks a turning point for Braze, indicating a recovery from previous challenges related to overbuying during the pandemic years of 2020-2022. The company’s strategic investments in product development are beginning to yield returns, showcasing the effectiveness of its offerings in the competitive AI marketing landscape.

The overall sentiment among analysts remains overwhelmingly positive, with all 21 analysts covering Braze rating it as a buy or strong buy. This consensus reflects a strong belief in the company's growth potential and its ability to navigate the evolving market dynamics. As Braze continues to innovate and expand its market share, it positions itself as a formidable competitor in the AI marketing sector, attracting both investor interest and consumer demand.
social_text: Braze's stock surged 19% after reporting Q4 revenue of $205.2M, exceeding expectations. Goldman Sachs maintains a buy rating, lowering the price target to $40, indicating a 122% upside potential.

title: This athleisure giant is primed for a sharp move lower. How to trade it using options
summary:
Lululemon (LULU), once a standout performer in the post-pandemic retail landscape, is now facing significant challenges as macroeconomic conditions worsen. The ongoing conflict in Iran has triggered an oil shock, leading to renewed inflationary pressures just as consumer demand begins to wane. In a stagflationary environment, premium brands like Lululemon often struggle to maintain pricing power, prompting consumers to opt for lower-cost alternatives. This shift in consumer behavior, coupled with intensified competition from both budget-friendly brands and emerging premium players, has raised concerns about Lululemon's ability to sustain its growth trajectory.

The company's recent foray into the footwear market has added complexity to its business model without yet proving to be a substantial profit driver. While Lululemon has historically enjoyed strong margins, the current landscape suggests a potential shift from a high-margin growth narrative to one focused on margin reversion. The stock has been hovering around a critical long-term support level of $160, which has been tested multiple times since late 2025. However, with signs of institutional distribution and poor relative strength against the S&P 500, the risk of a breakdown below this support level is increasing.

If LULU breaks below $160, analysts predict a potential decline towards the $140 mark, which represents the next significant downside target. Despite maintaining a premium margin profile, the company's growth expectations are deteriorating, as evidenced by its forward P/E ratio of approximately 13.0x compared to the industry average of 19.9x. With expected EPS and revenue growth rates lagging behind industry averages, Lululemon's position in the market is becoming increasingly precarious as it navigates these challenging economic conditions.
social_text: Lululemon faces mounting challenges as macroeconomic pressures rise, with a critical support level at $160 under threat. A break could lead to a drop towards $140, reflecting deteriorating growth expectations and increased competition.

title: ByteDance’s new AI video generation model, Dreamina Seedance 2.0, comes to CapCut
summary:
ByteDance has officially launched its new AI video generation model, Dreamina Seedance 2.0, within its popular editing platform, CapCut. This innovative model allows users to create and edit video content by utilizing prompts, images, or reference videos, streamlining the video production process for creators. The phased rollout will initially target users in select countries, including Brazil, Indonesia, Malaysia, Mexico, the Philippines, Thailand, and Vietnam, with plans to expand to additional markets in the future. This strategic move comes as OpenAI has recently scaled back its presence in the video generation space, particularly with the discontinuation of its Sora app, highlighting ByteDance's commitment to advancing its AI capabilities in the competitive landscape of content creation.

The Dreamina Seedance 2.0 model is designed to enhance the creative process by enabling users to generate video clips of up to 15 seconds in length, supporting six different aspect ratios. It boasts advanced features that allow for realistic rendering of textures, movement, and lighting, which are crucial for producing high-quality video content. This model not only aids creators in editing and enhancing their footage but also provides a platform for testing ideas based on initial concepts or sketches before committing to full production. The versatility of the model allows it to cater to various content types, including cooking tutorials, fitness videos, and business overviews, addressing challenges that AI video models have historically faced.

Despite the excitement surrounding the launch, the rollout has been somewhat limited due to ongoing concerns regarding intellectual property rights, particularly in relation to criticisms from Hollywood regarding potential copyright infringements. As ByteDance navigates these challenges, the company remains focused on expanding the capabilities of Dreamina Seedance 2.0 and ensuring compliance with legal standards. The introduction of this model within CapCut signifies a significant step forward for ByteDance in the realm of AI-driven content creation, positioning the company as a key player in the evolving landscape of digital media production.
social_text: ByteDance's Dreamina Seedance 2.0 launches in CapCut, enabling creators to generate 15-second videos using prompts or images. Initial rollout in Brazil, Indonesia, and more, amid IP concerns from Hollywood.

title: Discover Q1 2026 rewards calendar — Earn 5% cash back at grocery stores, wholesale clubs, and on streaming
summary:
Discover Financial Services has announced its cash-back rewards calendar for the first quarter of 2026, allowing cardholders to earn 5% cash back on specific categories. From January 1 to March 31, eligible users can benefit from this promotion at grocery stores, wholesale clubs, and select streaming services. This initiative is designed to encourage spending in everyday categories, making it easier for consumers to save money while managing their household expenses.

The grocery store category includes a wide range of eligible retailers, from supermarkets to smaller grocery outlets and delivery services. However, it's important to note that purchases made at Walmart and Target will not qualify for the cash-back offer. This promotion aligns with many consumers' New Year's resolutions to eat healthier and cook more at home, providing an incentive to shop for groceries.

In addition to grocery shopping, cardholders can also earn 5% cash back at wholesale clubs, although they must ensure that their Discover card is accepted at these locations. Notably, some services at wholesale clubs, such as travel or cell phone purchases, may not qualify for the cash-back rewards. This strategic move by Discover aims to enhance customer loyalty and increase card usage across essential spending categories, ultimately benefiting both the company and its customers.
social_text: Discover's Q1 2026 rewards offer 5% cash back on groceries, wholesale clubs, and select streaming services, up to $1,500 in purchases. Activate your eligible Discover card to maximize savings this quarter.

title: Cold storage trough in sight as vacancies hit 20-year high
summary:
The U.S. cold storage market is experiencing a significant downturn, primarily driven by an unprecedented surge in new facility construction and a slowdown in consumer spending. This has resulted in vacancy rates reaching a 20-year high, as the market grapples with the repercussions of pandemic-induced overbuilding and declining food inventory trends. Despite these challenges, the market has shown signs of resilience, with approximately 3.5 million square feet of positive absorption recorded in 2025, indicating a solid underlying demand for cold storage solutions, according to a recent report from Newmark.

The report highlights several ongoing challenges that are shaping the near-term outlook for the cold storage sector. Persistently high food prices are straining consumer budgets, leading to slowed consumption growth. Additionally, inventory carrying costs have surged, with rents doubling since 2020 and interest rates remaining elevated. As a result, while the supply-demand gap is expected to narrow this year due to a moderation in the development pipeline, supply is likely to continue outpacing absorption in the short term as existing construction projects are completed.

Newmark's analysis points to a notable trend of 'flight to quality' within the market, creating a bifurcated landscape between new and older assets. Occupants are increasingly favoring build-to-suit projects that emphasize automation, energy efficiency, and high-throughput capabilities. This shift has led to a significant exodus from older facilities, which accounted for 73% of industry vacancies last year. While legacy locations faced a 7.6% vacancy rate, modern sites that were operational prior to the pandemic maintained a much lower vacancy rate of 2.7% in the fourth quarter. Despite the cyclical reset, the sector is supported by favorable long-term catalysts, including population growth and the expansion of domestic food production.
social_text: The U.S. cold storage market faces a 20-year high vacancy rate due to overbuilding and slowing consumer spending. Despite challenges, 3.5 million sq ft of positive absorption was noted in 2025, indicating solid demand.

title: Pony.ai shares slide as revenue falls despite robotaxi surge
summary:
Pony.ai, the autonomous vehicle developer, faced a significant decline in its stock value, dropping 13.6% following the release of its fourth-quarter financial results. Despite reporting a GAAP net profit of $75.5 million, the figure was largely inflated by a $132.5 million paper profit from trading securities. The company's core non-GAAP operating losses widened to $65.9 million as it aggressively invested in expanding its fleet. This financial performance was compounded by an 18% year-on-year decline in total revenue, which fell to $29.1 million, primarily due to a staggering 53% drop in licensing and applications revenue.

On a more positive note, Pony.ai's robotaxi segment demonstrated remarkable growth, with revenue from these services soaring by 160% to reach $6.7 million. The fare-charging revenue for robotaxi services surged over 500%, indicating a strong demand for their autonomous transport solutions. Additionally, revenue from robotruck services saw a slight increase to $13.1 million. CEO Dr. James Peng emphasized the company's achievements in 2025, including reaching breakeven unit economics in various Chinese cities and achieving a record daily net revenue of RMB394 per Gen-7 robotaxi in Shenzhen.

Pony.ai's fleet has expanded to over 1,400 vehicles as of March 25, 2026, with ambitious plans to exceed 3,000 by the end of the year. The company is also broadening its international presence, launching operations in Croatia, Hangzhou, and Changsha. A strategic partnership with Uber Technologies aims to introduce Europe’s first commercial robotaxi service in Zagreb. With $1.5 billion in cash at the end of 2025, Pony.ai is well-positioned for future growth despite current challenges in revenue generation.
social_text: Pony.ai shares fell 13.6% as Q4 revenue dropped 18% to $29.1M, despite a 160% surge in robotaxi revenue to $6.7M. The fleet expanded to 1,400 vehicles, with plans for 3,000 by year-end.

title: Tom Brady-backed telehealth firm eMed valued at over $2 billion in latest funding round
summary:
Telehealth firm eMed has successfully raised $200 million in its latest funding round, bringing its valuation to over $2 billion. This funding round, which is categorized as 'Series A', was led by Aon Consulting and included notable investors such as Tom Brady, who serves as the company's founding chief wellness officer. eMed is strategically positioned in the burgeoning GLP-1 market, which focuses on obesity and diabetes treatments. The company aims to address the financial barriers associated with GLP-1 drug coverage, which has been a significant hurdle for wider insurance adoption. 

Founded in 2020, eMed initially gained recognition during the COVID-19 pandemic by providing at-home testing solutions. Since then, the company has expanded its offerings to include diagnostics for various health conditions. However, eMed is now shifting its focus towards developing an agentic AI platform and implementing a new healthcare payment model designed to reduce costs for employers. This strategic pivot is expected to enhance eMed's competitive edge in the telehealth sector, particularly as the demand for GLP-1 treatments is projected to generate annual sales of $150 billion by the next decade.

CEO Linda Yaccarino emphasized that this funding round not only confirms eMed's momentum but also positions the company as a leader in population health management. By partnering with employers and government payers, eMed aims to tackle the escalating healthcare costs that have burdened many organizations. With the backing of high-profile investors and a clear strategic vision, eMed is poised to make significant strides in the telehealth industry, ultimately benefiting both employers and patients alike.
social_text: eMed raises $200M in Series A funding, valuing the telehealth firm at over $2B. Backed by Tom Brady, the company targets the $150B GLP-1 market, aiming to reduce employer healthcare costs with innovative AI solutions.

title: Daily Spotlight: Stocks Can Survive a Pullback
summary:
The stock market has recently experienced a pullback, defined as a decline of 5% from its peak, which occurred two weeks ago due to geopolitical tensions in Iran and a surge in oil prices. The S&P 500 index fell from its all-time high of 7,002 to just below the 6,500 mark. Historically, such pullbacks are common, averaging three occurrences per year, while corrections, which involve a 10% drop, happen about once annually. The most recent correction was linked to the uncertainty surrounding President Trump's tariff policies in the spring of 2025.

Despite these fluctuations, historical data suggests that stocks tend to recover from both pullbacks and corrections, with the average recovery time being one month for pullbacks and four months for corrections. The resilience of the market is often contingent on prevailing economic fundamentals, which currently indicate growth. Prior to the recent oil price spike, interest rates were on a downward trend, suggesting a stable economic environment that could support a rebound in stock prices.

Investors should remain cautious but optimistic, as the market has a proven track record of bouncing back from downturns. Understanding the cyclical nature of stock movements can help investors navigate these temporary setbacks. As the economy continues to grow, the potential for recovery remains strong, reinforcing the idea that pullbacks do not necessarily signal the end of a bull market or the onset of a bear market.
social_text: The S&P 500 recently fell 5% from its peak of 7,002, closing below 6,500, driven by the Iran conflict and rising oil prices. Historically, pullbacks occur thrice yearly, with recoveries averaging one month.

title: Use options to play a potential Invesco runup in wake of Janus Henderson deal
summary:
The recent developments in the asset management sector highlight a significant shift in market dynamics, particularly following Victory Capital's withdrawal from the bidding for Janus Henderson. This move leaves Janus Henderson to be acquired by General Catalyst and Trian, indicating a competitive landscape where firms are vying for valuable assets. Despite the modest acquisition price of 11.6 times forward earnings, it suggests that the asset management industry is currently undervalued compared to the broader market. The ongoing fee pressure, primarily driven by the rise of exchange-traded funds (ETFs), is a concern for traditional asset managers, yet the bidding war for Janus indicates a belief in the long-term value of these firms.

Invesco, with its substantial $2.26 trillion in assets under management, stands out as a key player in this evolving market. The firm’s diverse offerings, including the lucrative Invesco QQQ Trust, provide a competitive edge that enhances its valuation. The current trading price of Invesco reflects a significant discount compared to what private equity firms would pay to replicate its business model. This disparity underscores the potential for growth and value realization in the asset management sector, particularly for firms with a strong market position and diversified strategies.

Investors are considering options strategies to capitalize on Invesco's potential run-up, especially given the attractive dividend yield and the current market conditions. With implied volatility at a premium, structuring options trades can provide a way to benefit from the anticipated price movements without directly purchasing the stock. This approach allows investors to offset potential dividend losses while positioning themselves for future gains as the market continues to reassess the value of asset management firms amidst ongoing changes in investor preferences and market conditions.
social_text: Invesco, managing $2.26 trillion in assets, is trading at a significant discount amid a bidding war for Janus Henderson, suggesting undervaluation in the asset management sector. Explore options strategies to capitalize on this potential run-up.

title: AI humanoid robots will soon invade our economy. Jefferies gives stocks to play the coming wave
summary:
According to a recent report by Jefferies, humanoid robots are poised to become a significant growth opportunity in the automation sector over the next decade. The firm identifies three key drivers behind this anticipated boom: an aging population that is increasing the demand for supplemental labor, a declining interest among younger generations in traditional manufacturing jobs, and ongoing advancements in semiconductor and artificial intelligence technologies. Jefferies emphasizes that while initial adoption of humanoid robots will likely occur in commercial settings where return on investment is more easily defined, broader demographic trends and safety concerns will facilitate widespread acceptance in various sectors.

Among the companies highlighted as potential beneficiaries of this robotic revolution is Tesla, which has shifted its focus towards developing humanoid robots. Analyst Philippe Houchois has assigned a hold rating to Tesla with a price target of $300, noting that while the company has made strides in this area, specific details regarding large-scale applications remain vague. Tesla's significant resources and early entry into the market may provide it with a competitive advantage, particularly through its integrated design and manufacturing capabilities. However, uncertainty regarding the commercial viability of humanoid robots persists, as the industry grapples with defining large-scale applications.

Additionally, Jefferies analysts point out that the materials required for humanoid robots present growth opportunities for companies in the metals sector. With 70% of a humanoid robot's weight attributed to metal, firms like Freeport McMoRan, Alcoa, and Nucor could see increased demand for copper, aluminum, and steel, respectively. As the market for humanoid robots expands, these companies may benefit from the rising need for the raw materials essential for their production, further underscoring the interconnectedness of technological advancements and traditional industries in shaping the future economy.
social_text: Jefferies predicts humanoid robots will drive automation growth over the next decade, with Tesla leading the charge. Analysts highlight Freeport McMoRan, Alcoa, and Nucor as key players in the metals supply chain for this emerging market.

title: Amazon's OpenAI deal will drive AI revenue growth, stock upside, Citi says
summary:
Amazon's recent partnership with OpenAI is set to significantly enhance its revenue growth and stock performance, according to a report from Citi Research. The investment bank has raised its price target for Amazon's stock from $265 to $285, indicating a potential upside of approximately 27% from its recent closing price. This optimistic outlook is largely driven by the increasing demand for artificial intelligence (AI) services and the anticipated revenue contributions from both OpenAI and Anthropic, alongside Amazon's core workloads. Citi's analyst Ronald Josey emphasized that the strategic collaboration with OpenAI, which includes a commitment of up to $50 billion in AI investments, positions Amazon Web Services (AWS) as a leading player in the AI infrastructure market.

The partnership designates AWS as the exclusive third-party cloud distributor for OpenAI Frontier, a platform designed for the development and management of AI agents. This agreement is part of a broader trend where major tech companies, including Meta, Oracle, Google, and Microsoft, are making substantial investments in AI infrastructure. Citi forecasts that AWS's revenue growth will experience a remarkable acceleration, projecting a 28% year-over-year increase in the first quarter of 2026, followed by 29% growth in the same year and an impressive 37% growth in 2027. AI revenue is expected to contribute significantly to AWS's overall revenue, accounting for around 58% of its incremental revenue in 2026 and potentially rising to 72% in 2027.

Despite some concerns regarding AWS's return on investment and competition, Citi believes that the demand for AI services will enable AWS to effectively ramp up its infrastructure capacity.
